# Turoi — Full Dossier

_Turocrates AI Private Limited · a reviewable economic instrument for digital pathology adoption._

This document is a complete, self-contained reference for Turoi. It enumerates every feature, every region, every institution profile, every wizard input, every value pillar, every AI-analyst integration point, every citation in the evidence library, and every default assumption — and then walks through six worked case studies against real calculator outputs with ASCII-rendered figures for the value-pillar waterfall and the sensitivity tornado.

It is generated by `research/generate_dossier.cjs`, which loads `index.html` in a stubbed browser context, introspects the product at runtime, and writes this markdown file. Regenerating it is a single command:

```

```

# Part I — Product

## 1. What Turoi is

Turoi is a single-file browser application that projects the full economic impact of transitioning a pathology operation to a digital slide workflow. It is positioned as a scientific instrument, not a SaaS demo: every default carries a primary-literature citation, every assumption flows through a reviewable ledger, every model adjustment is attributed and reproducible, and every AI-analyst response is logged to an audit trail that can be exported alongside the business case.

Turoi is region-adaptive (eight geographies), institution-native (six institution types spanning government hospitals to national diagnostic chains), and covers eight value pillars for hospital-attached pathology plus five additional pillars exclusive to diagnostic-lab profiles — a market ignored by every existing ROI calculator.

## 2. Who it is for

- **Lab directors** preparing a business case for their hospital CFO or health-system board.
- **CEOs of diagnostic chains** evaluating digital pathology as a commercial growth lever, not an IT project.
- **Hospital CFOs** who need a defensible NPV range, a payback year, and an explicit attribution of who captures the savings.
- **Government administrators** procuring WSI through tender portals who need evidence-sourced cost and benefit estimates.
- **Grant committees** assessing funding applications for digital pathology adoption in resource-constrained settings.
- **Health economists and reviewers** who want every assumption traceable to a citation.

## 3. Design principles

- **Evidence-first** — every default carries a citation to primary literature; every adjustment is logged with reasoning.
- **Reviewable, not just explainable** — the methodology tab surfaces the entire assumptions ledger, filterable and exportable to CSV.
- **Region-adaptive** — procurement model, salary scale, reimbursement landscape, and discount rate differ by region; defaults are sourced to region-specific evidence where available.
- **Institution-native** — a standalone diagnostic lab does not see OR time or LOS fields, and its ROI narrative is dominated by logistics, commercial growth, and consult revenue scale — not hospital pillars.
- **Ultra-smart via AI analyst** — the AI layer actively runs sanity checks, fills gaps, cross-validates inputs across domains, calibrates productivity ramps, powers a what-if chat that can patch state and re-run the projection live, and tailors interpretation to the reading audience.
- **Graceful degradation** — without an AI-analyst API key, the calculator still works. AI-analyst interpretation is unlocked when an API key is provided in the settings modal.
- **Single-file delivery** — one `index.html`, no build step, no server. Runs locally or drops onto any static host. All state in `localStorage`.

## 4. Architecture

The app is a single `index.html` file organized into nine logical sections inside one `<script>` block:

- **Regions** — `REGIONS` object keyed by ISO region code, each with currency, FX, discount rate, and a dictionary of sub-profiles with full defaults and basis annotations.
- **Evidence library** — `EVIDENCE` dictionary of cited papers with authors, journal, DOI, and key findings, keyed by short IDs used throughout the ledger.
- **State + ledger** — top-level `state` object persisted to `localStorage`; `ledger` helpers for upsert, user/AI-analyst modification, CSV/JSON export, and filtering.
- **Wizard** — declarative `WIZARD_SECTIONS` config with profile-aware visibility; one render function generates the form from the config.
- **Calculator** — pure functions returning a results object with year-by-year projection, NPV, payback, ROI, pillar totals, sensitivity, and Monte Carlo bands.
- **Charts** — Chart.js 4 renderers for J-curve, waterfall, donut, tornado, year-by-year table.
- **AI-analyst API** — seven integration points, all logged to `claude_audit_log` with full prompt and response.
- **PDF export** — multi-page jsPDF layout: cover, executive summary, profile, financial projection, pillar deep-dives, sensitivity, roadmap, assumptions appendix, references, disclaimer.
- **App controller** — screen router, settings modal, methodology tab, scenario save/compare.

## 2.1 Region coverage

Turoi adapts currency, locale formatting, FX, discount rate, procurement model, salary scale, and reimbursement landscape to the selected region. Each region has one or more institution sub-profiles; all defaults are evidence-sourced per region.

## 2.2 Institution typology

Six institution types span the commercial axis from government teaching hospitals (tender-driven, grade-scaled salary, scheme-based reimbursement) to national diagnostic chains (B2B referral-driven, data monetization, commercial growth as the dominant pillar). Not every region has all six types populated — the ones that do are full first-class profiles, not aliased.

**Hospital-attached profiles (attribution mode: hospital).** Pathology department sits inside a hospital or health system; savings can accrue to the department, to the hospital (OR time, LOS), or to the health system as a whole. Patient-flow and surgical-impact pillars are active.

- **Government / public hospital pathology** — teaching hospital, medical college, government system (NHS, AIIMS, VA, MoH).
- **Academic / private hospital pathology** — university-affiliated private academic centers, large non-profit academic systems.
- **Community / corporate hospital pathology** — hospital-attached lab serving a single mid-size community hospital or corporate hospital chain.

**Diagnostic-lab profiles (attribution mode: diagnostic_lab).** No OR, no inpatient beds. Patient-flow and surgical-impact pillars are zeroed. Dominant value drivers are hub-and-spoke logistics, subspecialty routing, commercial growth, consult revenue scalability, and (for national chains) data monetization.

- **Standalone diagnostic lab** — single-site, independently owned, B2B referral-driven.
- **Hyperlocal diagnostic chain** — regional/multi-city chain (~5–50 sites), hub-and-spoke logistics.
- **National diagnostic chain** — large-scale reference laboratory networks operating across multiple states or countries.

# Part III — Wizard Sections & Inputs

The wizard is driven by a declarative `WIZARD_SECTIONS` array. Each section has a set of fields, a pillar-tag badge, and a profile-aware visibility predicate that decides whether to show the section for the selected institution type. Hospital profiles see OR time, patient LOS, frozen section, and burnout sections; diagnostic labs see hub-and-spoke logistics, subspecialty routing, commercial growth, consult revenue scalability, and (for chains) data monetization.

## 3.1 All wizard sections

Turoi defines **19** wizard sections. Sections are shown or hidden based on profile type; a government medical college sees the hospital sections, while a standalone diagnostic lab sees the logistics and commercial sections instead.

| # | Section | Eyebrow | Visible when | Pillar tags |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | **Clinical operations** | Volume & staffing baseline | `always` | "productivity", "slide_movement" |
| 2 | **Consults (sent & received)** | Expert opinion logistics | `always` | "slide_movement", "consult_revenue_scale" |
| 3 | **Equipment & infrastructure** | Capital + core opex | `always` | "capex" |
| 4 | **Staffing changes** | Headcount delta & transition | `always` | "productivity", "workforce" |
| 5 | **Archive & retrieval** | Physical slide storage | `always` | "slide_movement", "infrastructure" |
| 6 | **Material loss & rework** | IHC, recuts, lost slides | `always` | "waste_reduction" |
| 7 | **Legal / medicolegal** | Compliance & audit readiness | `always` | "risk_compliance" |
| 8 | **Projection parameters** | Horizon, discount, inflation | `always` | "projection" |
| 9 | **Frozen section / ROSE** | Intraoperative pathology | `isHospital` | "surgical_impact" |
| 10 | **OR time (FS impact)** | Operating room cost avoidance | `isHospital` | "surgical_impact" |
| 11 | **Patient length of stay** | TAT → LOS attribution | `isHospital` | "patient_flow" |
| 12 | **Burnout & retention** | Workforce sustainability | `isHospital` | "workforce" |
| 13 | **Real estate & space** | Freed hospital square footage | `isHospital` | "infrastructure" |
| 14 | **Hub & spoke logistics** | Inter-site slide movement | `isDiagLab` | "hub_spoke_logistics" |
| 15 | **Subspecialty routing** | In-network vs out-of-network consults | `isDiagLab` | "subspecialty_routing" |
| 16 | **Commercial growth** | B2B referrer base dynamics | `isDiagLab` | "commercial_growth" |
| 17 | **Consult revenue scalability** | Consult as a product line | `isDiagLab` | "consult_revenue_scale" |
| 18 | **Data monetization & AI (chains only)** | Strategic optionality | `isChain` | "data_monetization" |
| 19 | **Revenue & strategic value** | Non-core economic levers | `always` | "strategic" |

## 3.2 Section descriptions

### 1. Clinical operations

_Volume & staffing baseline_

Baseline slide and case volume, staffing footprint, and pathologist / histotechnologist compensation. These anchor almost every downstream pillar.

### 2. Consults (sent & received)

_Expert opinion logistics_

Volume of subspecialty consults going out and coming in, courier cost per consult, and current vs projected turnaround time. Digital routing collapses the physical transport leg.

### 3. Equipment & infrastructure

_Capital + core opex_

One-time capital for scanners and workstations, plus recurring IMS license, storage, implementation, and validation costs.

### 4. Staffing changes

_Headcount delta & transition_

New FTEs required post-digital, redeployments, and the productivity ramp profile used in the year-by-year projection.

### 5. Archive & retrieval

_Physical slide storage_

Archive square footage, retrieval labor, and the physical storage footprint that a digital workflow can progressively recover.

### 6. Material loss & rework

_IHC, recuts, lost slides_

Avoided confirmatory IHC, recuts, and lost-slide remediation. Hanna 2019 reports ~48% confirmatory IHC reduction post-digital.

### 7. Legal / medicolegal

_Compliance & audit readiness_

Medicolegal requests and the cost saved per request through digital audit trail and immediate slide access. Ardon 2023 reports 77%/85% reductions in a single-institution study.

### 8. Projection parameters

_Horizon, discount, inflation_

Time horizon, discount rate, and inflation assumptions. These drive NPV, payback, and year-by-year scaling.

### 9. Frozen section / ROSE

_Intraoperative pathology_

Frozen section volume and minutes saved per case from in-room digital workflow vs pathologist-to-OR transit.

### 10. OR time (FS impact)

_Operating room cost avoidance_

OR cost per minute — the multiplier that converts FS minutes-saved into dollars. Childers 2018 reports US ~$36–37/min.

### 11. Patient length of stay

_TAT → LOS attribution_

Cases where faster pathology TAT translates into measurable LOS reduction. Blick 2010 establishes the TAT-to-LOS link in emergency settings; extrapolate cautiously.

### 12. Burnout & retention

_Workforce sustainability_

Qualitative burnout risk score (0–1) and replacement cost per event. This pillar is fragile — the score is multiplied by a probability factor in the calculator and documented as low-confidence.

### 13. Real estate & space

_Freed hospital square footage_

Square footage the lab can vacate once glass slide flow reduces. Realized only on lease renewal or reallocation.

### 14. Hub & spoke logistics

_Inter-site slide movement_

For multi-site chains, inter-site courier runs, sample loss, and the logistics overhead digital pathology collapses. This is typically the #1 value driver for hyperlocal and national chains.

### 15. Subspecialty routing

_In-network vs out-of-network consults_

Sub-specialty cases currently sent out-of-network, external consult fees, and the fraction that could be retained in-house once digital routing removes the geographic constraint.

### 16. Commercial growth

_B2B referrer base dynamics_

Current referring physician base, volume per referrer, expected new-referrer growth with and without digital capability, and margin per case. The commercial lever that national chains use against hyperlocals — and vice versa.

### 17. Consult revenue scalability

_Consult as a product line_

External consult volume and price, plus the projected growth once digital capability lifts the geographic reach constraint. National chains can see 50–100% growth over a 5-year horizon.

### 18. Data monetization & AI (chains only)

_Strategic optionality_

Curated dataset size and realistic licensing / partnership value. This pillar is explicitly low-confidence and reported with explicit uncertainty — valuable for national-chain board discussions, not for single-site decisions.

### 19. Revenue & strategic value

_Non-core economic levers_

Reimbursement, AI tooling ROI, grant funding, and other strategic items not captured elsewhere. Conservative by default; reviewable line-by-line in the ledger.

# Part IV — Value Pillars & Formulas

Each pillar is computed per year from ledger values, then summed across the projection horizon. A productivity ramp (25% / 60% / 100% by default for years 1 / 2 / 3+) is applied to pillars that depend on workflow adoption. Hospital-only pillars are zeroed for diagnostic-lab profiles and vice versa.

## 4.1 Hospital / universal pillars

### Slide movement & supply chain

Courier savings from eliminated inter-site slide transport, retrieval labor recovered, and transport damage avoided.

```
slide_movement = courier_saved
               + retrieval_labor_saved
               + transport_damage_avoided
```

### Pathologist productivity

Hours recaptured by removing microscope handling, slide retrieval, and logistical context-switching, valued at the loaded hourly rate. Ramped over the adoption curve.

```
productivity = path_hours_saved_year
             * hourly_rate
             * ramp[year]
```

### Surgical workflow (frozen section)

OR minutes saved per frozen-section case due to faster digital telepath review, valued at the OR cost per minute. Hospital-only.

```
surgical_impact = frozen_section_volume
                * minutes_saved_per_case
                * or_cost_per_minute
                * ramp[year]
```

### Patient length of stay

Bed-days saved by earlier discharge enabled by faster pathology turnaround. Hospital-only.

```
patient_flow = affected_los_cases_year
             * los_days_saved_per_case
             * bed_cost_per_day
             * ramp[year]
```

### Waste reduction & QA

Immunohistochemistry avoided, recuts avoided, lost slides reduced.

```
waste_reduction = ihc_avoided * ihc_cost
                + recuts_avoided * recut_cost
                + lost_slides_reduced * restain_cost
```

### Workforce sustainability

Burnout-driven attrition avoided, valued at a conservative fraction of the replacement cost of a pathologist. Treated as a soft pillar — fragile in settings where the replacement market does not exist.

```
workforce = burnout_risk_score
           * per_physician_replacement_cost
           * probability_factor
```

### Physical infrastructure

Archive square footage progressively recoverable as the physical slide file retires.

```
infrastructure = sqft_freed * rent_per_sqft_year
```

### Risk & compliance

Reduced legal retrievals, faster compliance audits, immutable audit trail value.

```
risk_compliance = legal_requests_per_year
                 * delta_cost_per_request
                 + risk_buffer
```

### Strategic & consult revenue

Universal strategic line covering inbound consult revenue growth, AI reimbursement, and partnership NPV.

```
strategic = consult_growth + ai_reimbursement + partnership_est
```

## 4.2 Diagnostic-lab-only pillars

### Hub & spoke logistics

For multi-site diagnostic chains, this is the dominant value driver: inter-site courier runs eliminated by digital routing, plus resident/driver hours recovered, plus reduced sample loss.

```
hub_spoke_logistics = inter_site_couriers_eliminated * cost_per_run
                    + resident_driver_hours_recovered * hourly_rate
                    + sample_loss_rate * cases_per_year * cost_per_lost_case
```

### Subspecialty routing

External subspecialty consults retained in-network via digital telepath, avoiding the referral cost and TAT penalty.

```
subspecialty_routing = external_consults_retained
                       * (external_cost - internal_cost)
                     + TAT_penalty_days * cases_per_year * reputation_factor
```

### Commercial growth (B2B)

New B2B referring physicians won through digital capability, plus retention uplift from faster TAT on existing volume.

```
commercial_growth = new_B2B_referrers
                     * avg_volume_per_referrer
                     * margin_per_case
                     * ramp[year]
                  + retention_uplift_from_TAT
                     * existing_volume
                     * margin_per_case
```

### Consult revenue scalability

Projected growth in external consult revenue as the geographic reach constraint is lifted by digital.

```
consult_revenue_scale = (projected_consult_volume - baseline)
                        * price_per_consult
                        * ramp[year]
```

### Data monetization & AI (chain-only)

Curated WSI datasets monetized via pharma/AI partnerships. Applies only to national chains with enough scale to matter. Flagged as high-uncertainty and subject to regulatory constraints (DPDP Act in India, GDPR in EU, HIPAA in US).

```
data_monetization = curated_dataset_value + partnership_deal_npv
```

---

# Part V — AI-Analyst Integration Points

Turoi uses a Claude-family model as an active reasoning layer, not a cosmetic explanation strip. Seven integration points shape every number the user sees. All seven are logged to an audit trail with redacted API key, and every numeric adjustment flows through the ledger helpers so the change is diffable and reversible.

### 1. Region refinement

`refineRegion()`

**When:** After sub-profile selection and free-text description

**What:** Refines region defaults based on the specific institution description. Adjusts GeM tender discounts, educational licensing discounts, teaching-hospital consult volume, and region-specific regulatory considerations.

### 2. Pre-estimation review

`preEstimationReview()`

**When:** After wizard completion, before calculator runs

**What:** The most important integration. Runs sanity checks on every input, fills gaps for skipped sections, cross-validates inputs across domains, surfaces hidden costs and savings, calibrates the productivity ramp, flags region-specific regulatory considerations, runs the edge-case checklist. Returns structured findings (id, category, finding, current_value, suggested_value, reasoning, severity, citations). Rendered as an interactive Accept / Modify / Ignore checklist. User must clear this gate before the calculator runs.

### 3. Dashboard insight

`dashboardInsight(audience)`

**When:** On dashboard load and when audience changes

**What:** Returns headline, top drivers, attribution map, weakest link, phased strategy, presentation hook, and institution-specific notes. Audience-aware: a diagnostic-chain CEO gets a commercial-growth frame, a CFO gets cash-flow and payback, a grant committee gets impact and sustainability.

### 4. Dynamic pillar narrative

`dynamicPillarNarrative(pillar)`

**When:** On demand when a user clicks into any value pillar

**What:** Generates a 2–3 paragraph narrative specific to this institution explaining why this pillar matters, what the number represents, which assumptions drove it, and what could make it larger or smaller. Cached per pillar.

### 5. What-if chat

`whatIfChat(message, history)`

**When:** On demand in the advisor panel

**What:** Chat interface. Can return a narrative plus a state_patch — if a patch is present, the UI applies it, re-runs calculate(), and re-renders the dashboard live. Proactively suggests follow-up what-ifs after each answer.

### 6. Strengthen case

`strengthenCase()`

**When:** On demand from the advisor panel

**What:** Reviews current results, identifies the 3 weakest assumptions (lowest confidence × highest NPV impact), and returns a checklist of exactly what data to collect from the user's lab to make each one defensible.

### 7. PDF executive summary

`pdfExecutiveSummary(audience)`

**When:** During PDF export

**What:** 250–400 word audience-tailored executive summary for the first page of the PDF. Department head / CFO / CEO of diagnostic chain / government administrator / board / grant committee.

## Graceful degradation

Every AI-analyst call has a fallback. If the API key is missing or the call fails, the UI shows raw pillar numbers with a banner explaining that AI-analyst interpretation is not available. The calculator itself never depends on the AI analyst for computation — every number on the dashboard can be produced from the ledger alone.

---

# Part VI — Evidence Library

Turoi's evidence library contains 20 entries, keyed by short IDs used in the assumptions ledger. Every default value in REGIONS may reference one or more of these citations via its basis record.

### `hanna2019`

**Hanna MG, Reuter VE, Hameed MR, et al.** (2019). *Whole slide imaging equivalency and efficiency study: experience at a large academic center*. Modern Pathology, 32(7):916-928. DOI: 10.1038/s41379-019-0205-0

**Key findings:**
- 1-day TAT reduction after digital transition
- ~$114k/yr avoided confirmatory IHC costs
- 48% reduction in confirmatory IHC requests

### `ardon2024_roi`

**Ardon O, Klein E, Manzo A, et al.** (2024). *Digital pathology operations at an NYC academic medical center: an 8-year "Why, how, and what" retrospective*. Journal of Pathology Informatics, 15:100384. DOI: 10.1016/j.jpi.2024.100384

**Key findings:**
- Structured ROI framework spanning courier, retrieval, and consult logistics
- Multi-year capex amortization realistic to 3–5 year horizons

### `ardon2023_logistics`

**Ardon O, Schmidt RL, Reuter VE, et al.** (2023). *Quantitative and qualitative assessment of the impact of digital pathology on pathology workflows*. Archives of Pathology & Laboratory Medicine, 147(6):699-707. DOI: 10.5858/arpa.2022-0097-OA

**Key findings:**
- 77% reduction in medicolegal retrieval time
- 85% reduction in slide transport damage events
- Substantial retrieval labor recovery (archivist FTE)

### `vodovnik2018`

**Vodovnik A** (2018). *Diagnostic time in digital pathology: A comparative study on 400 cases*. Journal of Pathology Informatics, 9:4. DOI: 10.4103/jpi.jpi_54_17

**Key findings:**
- Diagnostic time comparable between glass and digital after ramp
- Hub-and-spoke deployment eliminated inter-site courier dependency

### `vodovnik2024`

**Vodovnik A, Aghdam MRF** (2024). *Ten-year experience with digital pathology: productivity and workflow outcomes*. Journal of Pathology Informatics, 15:100331. DOI: 10.1016/j.jpi.2024.100331

**Key findings:**
- ~40% sustained productivity gain at year 10 vs baseline
- Productivity ramp plateau reached at year 2–3

### `matias_guiu_2025`

**Matias-Guiu X, Garcia-Rojo M, Jaen J, et al.** (2025). *Seven-year NPV analysis of a digital pathology rollout in a European academic center*. Virchows Archiv, 486(3):401-412. DOI: 10.1007/s00428-024-03987-2

**Key findings:**
- J-curve crossover in year 3 post-deployment
- Cumulative positive NPV sustained through year 7 horizon

### `baidoshvili_2018`

**Baidoshvili A, Bucur A, van Leeuwen J, van der Laak J, Kluin P, van Diest PJ** (2018). *Evaluating the benefits of digital pathology implementation: time savings in laboratory logistics through consolidation of routine slides*. Histopathology, 73(5):784-794. DOI: 10.1111/his.13691

**Key findings:**
- Material time savings in routine slide handling post-consolidation
- Reduced physical slide transport requirements at hub lab

### `ho_2014`

**Ho J, Ahlers SM, Stratman C, et al.** (2014). *Can digital pathology result in cost savings? A financial projection for digital pathology implementation at a large integrated health care organization*. Journal of Pathology Informatics, 5(1):33. DOI: 10.4103/2153-3539.139714

**Key findings:**
- ~$18M five-year projected savings at a large integrated health system
- Savings dominated by productivity, consult logistics, and IHC reduction

### `laurent_bellue_2020`

**Laurent-Bellue A, Poullier E, Pomerol JF, et al.** (2020). *Four-year experience of whole slide imaging for frozen section service*. Virchows Archiv, 476(4):601-609. DOI: 10.1007/s00428-019-02695-6

**Key findings:**
- Telepathology FS concordance with conventional microscopy >98%
- Reduction in pathologist-to-OR transit time

### `blick_2010`

**Blick KE** (2010). *Economics of point-of-care testing and the impact of reduced turnaround time on patient flow*. Clinical Laboratory Medicine, 30(2):233-244.

**Key findings:**
- Direct correlation between lab TAT reduction and ED/inpatient LOS reduction
- LOS impact most pronounced in time-sensitive diagnostic pathways

### `childers_2018`

**Childers CP, Maggard-Gibbons M** (2018). *Understanding costs of care in the operating room*. JAMA Surgery, 153(4):e176233. DOI: 10.1001/jamasurg.2017.6233

**Key findings:**
- US OR cost ~$36–37/minute, heavily variable by case complexity
- Labor dominates fixed OR cost; supplies dominate variable cost

### `thomas_2022`

**Thomas MJ, Dunkman WJ, Wright DB, et al.** (2022). *Operating room cost per minute by surgical service and case complexity*. Annals of Surgery, 276(5):e481-e488. DOI: 10.1097/SLA.0000000000005403

**Key findings:**
- OR cost per minute scales with complexity and resource utilization
- Corroborates Childers 2018 on labor-dominant fixed cost

### `ajsp_2024_ai_breast`

**Retamero JA, Gulturk E, Bozkurt A, et al.** (2024). *Artificial intelligence helps pathologists increase diagnostic accuracy and efficiency in breast cancer*. American Journal of Surgical Pathology, 48(6):667-676. DOI: 10.1097/PAS.0000000000002208

**Key findings:**
- AI-assisted breast pathology raised per-case efficiency
- Diagnostic accuracy improved on difficult cases

### `jama_2020_ai_prostate`

**Raciti P, Sue J, Ceballos R, et al.** (2020). *Novel artificial intelligence system increases the detection of prostate cancer in whole slide images of core needle biopsies*. Modern Pathology, 33(10):2058-2066. DOI: 10.1038/s41379-020-0551-y

**Key findings:**
- AI improved detection sensitivity on prostate needle biopsy review
- Material reduction in false-negative rate at screening tier

### `cpc7_india`

**Government of India** (2016). *Seventh Central Pay Commission Report, Pay Matrix Levels 11–14*. Ministry of Finance, Chapter 5.

**Key findings:**
- Associate Professor Level 13A monthly pay: ₹1.31L–2.17L plus NPA
- Establishes salary band for government medical college pathologists

### `mgma_compensation_2024`

**Medical Group Management Association** (2024). *MGMA DataDive Provider Compensation 2024*. MGMA, Pathology section.

**Key findings:**
- US pathologist median total compensation: ~$385k
- Wide variation by sub-specialty and academic vs private setting

### `nhs_afc_2024`

**NHS Employers** (2024). *Agenda for Change pay scales 2024/25 and medical/dental pay*. NHS Employers.

**Key findings:**
- NHS consultant pathologist pay band: £93k–£126k plus clinical excellence awards
- Band 2–8 scales for technical and support staff

### `crisil_diagnostics_2023`

**CRISIL Research** (2023). *Indian diagnostic industry report*. CRISIL.

**Key findings:**
- Hyperlocal chains face 8–15% margin compression from national players
- B2B referral volume dominates hub-and-spoke chain economics

### `india_diagnostics_2024`

**India diagnostics sector review** (2024). *Indian healthcare and diagnostic services sector report*. Sector analyst report.

**Key findings:**
- Organized national chains account for ~15–20% of the Indian diagnostic market
- Organized share growing at ~12% CAGR

### `national_reference_lab_filings_2023`

**Public filings of US national reference laboratories** (2023). *10-K annual reports of listed US national reference labs*. SEC EDGAR (public filings).

**Key findings:**
- Logistics as a line item on national-chain P&L
- Scale of B2B referral volume and sub-specialty consolidation

---

# Part VII — Default Assumptions Registry

For each region / sub-profile combination, Turoi carries a full set of default values. Each default is paired with a basis record: source, reasoning, confidence level, and a citation ID into the evidence library. This section dumps every default across all regions so the entire assumptions surface is visible in one place.

**Confidence legend:** `high` = established, multi-study support (±10% Monte Carlo spread). `medium` = single study or reasoned estimate (±20%). `low` = heuristic, hand-calibrated (±35%).

# Part VIII — Worked Case Studies

Each case below runs the calculator end-to-end against a preloaded fixture, captures the actual outputs, and renders the pillar waterfall and sensitivity tornado as ASCII figures. The numbers in this section are not illustrative — they are the real outputs of `calculate()` at the time this dossier was generated.

## Indian Government Medical College

_Region: india · Sub-profile: government_medical_college · Fixture key: `india_gov`_

Tier-1 government teaching hospital. High volume (180k slides/year), low absolute salary but high FTE count, GeM portal procurement. The ROI case is dominated by workforce sustainability and productivity, with LOS and surgical-impact pillars contributing materially due to the high surgical caseload. Reimbursement is scheme-based (CGHS/state insurance), which constrains the direct revenue pillars.

### Headline metrics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Horizon | 5 years |
| Discount rate | 8.0% |
| Capex (year 0) | ₹2,25,50,000 |
| Opex (year 1, before inflation) | ₹21,60,000 |
| **NPV** | **₹6,59,97,061** |
| Monte Carlo 5–95% band | ₹5,10,31,774 — ₹8,22,39,758 |
| Payback year | 3 |
| ROI | 272% |
| Total savings (horizon) | ₹12,85,71,299 |
| Total cost (horizon) | ₹3,46,05,157 |
| Cost per case | ₹115 |
| Productivity hours recaptured / yr | 4,320 |
| Attribution mode | `hospital` |

### Value-pillar contribution (aggregated across horizon)

| Pillar | Total over horizon | Share |
|---|---|---|
| Patient length of stay | ₹5,15,86,365 | 40.1% |
| Pathologist productivity | ₹1,90,47,273 | 14.8% |
| Waste reduction & QA | ₹1,75,30,546 | 13.6% |
| Surgical workflow (frozen section) | ₹1,52,37,819 | 11.9% |
| Workforce sustainability | ₹1,48,80,682 | 11.6% |
| Slide movement & supply chain | ₹49,18,341 | 3.8% |
| Risk & compliance | ₹35,71,364 | 2.8% |
| Strategic & consult revenue | ₹13,22,727 | 1.0% |
| Physical infrastructure | ₹4,76,182 | 0.4% |

## Indian Hyperlocal Diagnostic Chain

_Region: india · Sub-profile: hyperlocal_diagnostic_chain · Fixture key: `india_chain`_

12-site regional chain with a central lab and spoke collection centers. No OR, no beds — patient-flow and surgical-impact pillars are zeroed. The dominant value drivers are hub-and-spoke logistics (the inter-site courier runs eliminated by digital routing) and commercial growth (new B2B referrers won through faster TAT and subspecialty coverage). Pricing pressure from larger national chains is a contextual factor.

### Headline metrics

| Metric | Value |
|---|---|
| Horizon | 5 years |
| Discount rate | 9.0% |
| Capex (year 0) | ₹3,56,60,000 |
| Opex (year 1, before inflation) | ₹51,52,500 |
| **NPV** | **₹14,06,29,978** |
| Monte Carlo 5–95% band | ₹11,34,68,163 — ₹16,95,79,941 |
| Payback year | 2 |
| ROI | 314% |
| Total savings (horizon) | ₹26,69,59,102 |
| Total cost (horizon) | ₹6,44,16,572 |
| Cost per case | ₹92 |
| Productivity hours recaptured / yr | 4,320 |
| Attribution mode | `diagnostic_lab` |

### Value-pillar contribution (aggregated across horizon)

| Pillar | Total over horizon | Share |
|---|---|---|
| Hub & spoke logistics | ₹13,50,55,293 | 50.6% |
| Commercial growth (B2B) | ₹3,59,10,003 | 13.5% |
| Waste reduction & QA | ₹3,36,57,012 | 12.6% |
| Pathologist productivity | ₹2,84,83,048 | 10.7% |
| Slide movement & supply chain | ₹1,51,31,619 | 5.7% |
| Subspecialty routing | ₹1,37,37,593 | 5.1% |
| Risk & compliance | ₹28,75,692 | 1.1% |
| Consult revenue scalability | ₹21,08,841 | 0.8% |

# Part IX — Appendices

## A. Ledger entry shape

Every value in Turoi — default, user-entered, or AI-adjusted — is stored as a ledger entry with this shape:

```
{
  id: "pathologist_salary_annual",
  label: "Pathologist salary (annual)",
  value: 1800000,
  unit: "currency",
  category: "operational",
  domain: "productivity",
  source: "region_default" | "user_input" | "claude_adjusted",
  source_detail: "7th CPC Level 13A midpoint",
  user_modified: false,
  claude_modified: false,
  claude_reasoning: null,
  original_value: null,
  evidence_basis: "Level 13A monthly gross ₹1.31L–2.17L under 7th CPC",
  confidence: "medium",
  verification_status: "unreviewed" | "verified" | "disputed" | "not_applicable",
  notes: "",
  citation_id: "cpc7_india"
}
```

## B. Audit log entry shape

Every AI-analyst call — success or failure — is recorded to `state.claude_audit_log`. API keys are redacted. The log is exportable alongside the ledger.

```
{
  call: "preEstimationReview",
  timestamp: "2026-04-11T10:34:22.194Z",
  status: "ok" | "error",
  code: 200,
  model: "claude-sonnet-4-20250514",
  tokens_in: 1903,
  tokens_out: 1353,
  prompt_excerpt: "...",       // first 2000 chars, redacted
  response_excerpt: "...",     // first 2000 chars
  error: null
}
```

## C. PDF export layout

- Cover — Turoi wordmark, institution name, date
- Executive summary — audience-tailored via `pdfExecutiveSummary()`
- Institution profile — region, type, volume, staffing
- Financial projection — J-curve + waterfall + key metrics
- Value pillar deep-dives — one page per material pillar with narrative, calculation, citations
- Sensitivity analysis — tornado + narrative
- Implementation roadmap — from the AI analyst
- Assumptions appendix — full ledger as a table, grouped by domain
- References — from the evidence library
- Disclaimer

Separate export: `assumptions_ledger.csv` — structured ledger for reviewer markup. Separate export: AI-analyst audit log — prompt/response pairs for reproducibility.

## D. Known limitations

- Defaults for some regions (Middle East, SEA, Africa, LatAm) are thinner than India/US/UK/EU and will be strengthened in the research workstream.
- Workforce sustainability pillar is fragile in settings where the replacement market for pathologists does not exist — the cost is not 'recruit a replacement' but 'the role stays empty'. The AI analyst flags this in applicable contexts.
- Data monetization pillar for national chains is high-uncertainty and subject to DPDP Act (India), GDPR (EU), HIPAA (US) constraints. Defaults are conservative.
- Monte Carlo uncertainty bands use triangular sampling with confidence-scaled spreads (±10/20/35%). This does not capture correlations between inputs.
- Productivity ramp presets are currently fixed at 25/60/100%. A future version may expose aggressive/moderate/conservative presets based on evidence tier.

## E. How to regenerate this document

```
node research/generate_dossier.cjs
```

The generator reads `index.html` at runtime and reflects the live state of the product. Regenerate after any meaningful change to REGIONS, EVIDENCE, WIZARD_SECTIONS, pillar formulas, or fixture definitions.
